Class AbstractVariableStorage<ID>

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.variable.AbstractVariableStorage<ID>
Type Parameters:
ID - the type of variable ID
All Implemented Interfaces:
VariableStorage<ID>
Direct Known Subclasses:
Sprite, Stage
public abstract class AbstractVariableStorage<ID> extends Object implements VariableStorage<ID>
Provides an implementation of VariableStorage.

    • variables

      protected final Map<ID, Variable<?>> variables

      protected final Map<ID, Variable<?>> variablesView

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractVariableStorage

      protected AbstractVariableStorage()
      Constructor for subclasses to call.

  • Method Details

    • createVariables

      protected abstract Map<ID, Variable<?>> createVariables()
      Creates a new Map to store variables in. This method must not have side effects and must solely create a new empty mutable map instance (e.g., using constructor).
      Returns:
      a new variable map

    • getVariables

      public Map<ID, Variable<?>> getVariables()
      Returns an unmodifiable view over this storage's variables.
      Specified by:
      getVariables in interface VariableStorage<ID>
      Returns:
      an immutable view over registered variables

    • registerVariable

      public void registerVariable(ID id, Variable<?> variable)
      Registers a variable under the given ID.
      Specified by:
      registerVariable in interface VariableStorage<ID>
      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the variable
      variable - the variable
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if put operation is not supported by the backing map

    • getVariable

      public <T> Variable<T> getVariable(ID id)
      Returns a variable with the given ID. Equivalent to getVariables().get(id), except it can return a generically typed variable.
      Specified by:
      getVariable in interface VariableStorage<ID>
      Type Parameters:
      T - the requested variable type
      Parameters:
      id - the ID
      Returns:
      the respective variable
      Throws:
      ClassCastException - if the variable's actual type does not match the requested one. Note: Java erases generic types at runtime, so the error might appear only when an attempt is made to read or write the variable's value, not when the variable itself is retrieved.
